# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

# Create temporary files with predictable names 
tmpfile1="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp"
tmpfile2="/tmp/another-$$.tmp"

echo "Some data for the first temp file." > $tmpfile1
cat $tmpfile1

# Process the data from the temporary file (replace this with your actual processing code)
sed 's/data/processed_data/g' $tmpfile1 > $tmpfile2

# Read and display results
cat $tmpfile2
